title: Research on Control of an Electro-Hydraulic Active Suspension System
authors: Hongyu Ye, Lijun Zhang, Dejian Meng
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Intelligent Manufacturing (MAIM)
abstract: This paper conducts modeling and control studies on an active suspension system. The modeling approach involves formulating individual components of the active suspension system, including vehicle model, actuator model, two types of road profile models, lower-level controller design, and three distinct upperlevel controller designs. The results indicate that the performance metrics of the active suspension system equipped with the three upper-layer control algorithms are optimized under both road conditions compared with the passive suspension system.

title: An Intelligent Type-2 Fuzzy Logic Framework for High-Accuracy Power Quality Disturbance Classification
authors: Rahul, Fatima Ansari, Manoj Kumar Mukul, Yogesh Pratap, M. Gangadharappa, Pooja Devi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Application of power quality disturbance identification and classification is a basic requirement for reliable power system functioning. This work proposes an intelligent method that combines the Curvelet Transform with Type-2 Fuzzy Logic for improved classification results. The Curvelet Transform has superior time-frequency analysis capabilities due to its ability to efficiently extract characteristic disturbance patterns. Type-2 Fuzzy Logic handles multicriteria decision-making in uncertain situations. The proposed method is tested on real-world data sets that include harmonics, flicker, transients, voltage sags, and voltage swells. The experimental results show that the proposed framework has a classification accuracy of 99.7%, which is higher than many existing methods, including Eigen-based Type-2 Fuzzy Logic (99.08%), FPGAbased online detection <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(98.57 \%)$</tex>, Linear Discriminant Analysis (98%), and deep learning with 2D wavelet scalograms (97.67%). The accuracy is also comparable to the IAST methodology with Random Forest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.56 \%)$</tex>. The accuracy and low computational complexity of the proposed framework emphasize the efficiency and applicability of the Curvelet Transform-Type-2 Fuzzy Logic framework for automated power quality disturbance classification.

title: Federated Intrusion Detection for VANETs under Byzantine Model Poisoning Attacks
authors: Shivanshi Mishra, Shruti Singh, Kanchan Sharma, Brijesh Kumar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has drawn interest because of its decentralised and privacy-preserving features for intrusion detection in Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs). However, these systems are prone to Byzantine model poisoning attacks, in which hacked vehicles alter local updates to hinder global learning. The impact of gradient-scaling model poisoning on federated VANET intrusion detection under realistic non-IID data distribution and partial client participation is examined in this work. Several aggregation strategies, including Federated Averaging, Median, Trimmed Mean, and Krum, are evaluated for robustness against such attacks. The impact of false-negative cars on downstream routing is also measured by analyzing nodelevel network reliability indicators in addition to packet-level detection performance. The findings show that robust aggregation, especially Krum, significantly improves network stability and improves malicious detection.

title: A Drone Shape Substrate Integrated Waveguide Backed Self-Quardruplexing Antenna for C-Band Applications
authors: Ravi Bhushan Roy, Prashant Kumar Jain, Prabhat Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: In this paper a Drown shape simple SIW (Substrate Integrated Wave-guide) antenna with backed antenna with self-quadruplexing structure for four-band operation application is presented. Copper-filled cylindrical vias are placed through the substrate between the top and bottom cavity layers, forming a single printed circuit board. The SIW cavity is excited through four distinct patches having different lengths, and each one radiates at a unique resonant frequency. These patches are formed on the upper surface of the cavity. The antenna design follows a simple three-step process aimed at improving the self-quadruplexing feature. In the initial stage, a rectangular slot along with two ports is created Secondly, the same slot is redesigned into a drone-shaped structure incorporating four patches. In the third step, all four ports are created and radiates at four different resonant frequencies at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.23 \text{GHz}, 6.42 \text{GHz}, 7.41 \text{GHz}$</tex> & 8.48 GHz, with gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.2,5.2,6.4$</tex>, and 6.5 dBi respectively. The proposed structure has been simulated using ANSYS HFSS on RT-Duroid (5870) Substrate.

title: Intelligent and Optimized Fraud Detection Framework for UPI Transactions
authors: Akash Raghuvanshi, Satyam Singh, Pawan Kumar Singh, Manish Kumar, Rahul Singh, Gunjan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The Digital Payment sector witnessed a transformation in the last couple of years, in part due to the increase in the use of UPI Transactions in India. However, this also led to an increase in vulnerable, sophisticated, and complicated fraud schemes. In this paper, we propose an integration of Seagull Optimization Algorithm (SOA) and LightGBM, which can achieve desirable results in terms of accurately forecasting fraudulent UPI Transactions. In our method, we capture the fraud indicators using detailed feature engineering, which includes temporal patterns, transaction behavioral analytics, and transaction anomalies. The SOA metaheuristic algorithm optimizes the hyperparameters of LightGBM and outperforms traditional optimization methods in accuracy and performance. We demonstrate the model results using an experimental dataset of 100,000 transactions, achieving 98.08 % cross-validated AUC, 98.02 % test AUC, and 94.95% accuracy at the optimal decision threshold of 0.30. The model achieves high recall of 99.98 % while maintaining precision of 80 %, effectively balancing false positives and false negatives. We also showcase the importance of critical fraud detection indicators: Device_ID, Customer_ID, IP_Address, and Merchant_ID, demonstrating this research's contribution in creating an effective, real-time, and reliable fraud detection model for UPI Transactions in Digital Payment Systems.

title: Energy-Aware Hybrid Localization in IoT and WSNs Using Multilateration and Grey Wolf Optimization
authors: Jyoti Sharma, Ashish Payal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: For the seamless functioning of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), precise and energy-efficient localization is of utmost importance. Existing range-based localization approaches are able to ensure high accuracy but suffer from high energy incurred due to computational part, while existing range-free localization approaches provide low computational cost but suffer from low precision issues. This paper addresses these limitations and presents a novel hybrid approach to localization that incorporates rangefree approach, i.e. weighted centroid estimation, rangebased approach, i.e. Multilateration, and a bio-inspired meta-heuristic optimization approach, i.e. Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) to improve precision while keeping the computations as well as energy expenditure at a minimum. Multilateration is used as an initial estimates and tunable fusion parameter is used to balance weightedcentroid and Multilateration, final position estimates are then refined using GWO method. This work uses a simplified energy model for both computation and communication costs. Proposed hybrid model is evaluated against a baseline Weighted Grey Wolf Optimization (WGWO) algorithm, focusing on Root Mean Square localization error and energy efficiency that encompasses both computation and communication costs. The simulation results show that the proposed hybrid model achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \%}$</tex> lower localization RMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 - 1 0 \%}$</tex> lower total energy consumption in comparison of the WGWO baseline. Moreover, CDF analysis also confirms the hybrid model's enhanced dependability and accuracy. This research provides a substantial promise toward optimizing algorithms for localization within large-scale IoT and WSN systems, strategically manoeuvring the constraints of accuracy and energy consumption. These findings demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach for real-time localization in resource-constrained IoT and WSN systems.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Blockchain-Fog Vs Cloud-Based Models for Real-Time Health Monitoring
authors: Ashish Kumar, Aanchal Vij, Ashish Jain, Jitendra Singh, Mekhala, Kamini
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: In this study, we conducted a real-time evaluation of two healthcare monitoring architectures, one cloud-based and the other integrating fog and blockchain technologies. The cloud system is typically based on centralized processing servers, whereas the proposed architecture combines the fog devices and a permissioned blockchain to achieve secure real-time data handling at the network edge. Both models were implemented using NodeMCU microcontrollers and MAX30100 sensors for real-time physiological data recording, including heart rate and SpO<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. In fog-blockchain deployment, smart contracts are used for controlling data access, and 'off-chain storage' and it is supported by the Inter-Planetary File System (IPFS), to reduce blockchain overhead. To evaluate both setups, we measured three performance metrics such as latency, transaction throughput, and average CPU load

title: A Space-Efficient Multi Rotor Wind Turbine System for Decentralized Power
authors: Jawaharlal Bhukya, Somasri Yaswanth Reddy Alavala, Vivek Chandra Kodamasimham, Yaswanth Koyaneni, Yaswanth Koyaneni, Siddhesh Bhushan
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Reliable electricity supply in rural and remote areas remains challenging due to the intermittency of solar photovoltaic systems during low-irradiance conditions. This paper presents a compact, low-cut-in-speed multi-propeller wind energy system intended for effective operation in weak winds. The proposed system utilizes four eight-blade propellers arranged at 90° intervals and mechanically coupled via a planetary gearbox to a common generator shaft, allowing for efficient torque aggregation while maintaining a compact, rooftop-capable footprint. Prototype blades were 3D-printed in PLA with a radial span of 145.5 mm, a curvature length of 175 mm, and a 27° twist. Laboratory testing of the prototype shows a cut-in speed of approximately 2.8 m/s and a monotonic increase in generator speed, voltage, and output power with wind speed, achieving 12.73 W at 6.7 m/s under a 25Ω load. These results demonstrate the feasibility of the multi-propeller planetary configuration as a modular solution for low-wind rural electrification and hybrid renewable systems.

title: Intelligent Merging Unit Enabled Substation Automation: A Critical Survey and Laboratory Setup
authors: Gayatri Mohapatra, Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sarat Chandra Swain, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Buddhadeva Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The research efforts are being directed toward the future deployment of the Centralized Protection and Monitoring (CPM) System in substations due to the growing need for adaptable protection and control algorithms in the field of substation automation. Intelligent Electronic Devices (IED) are currently widely used in digital substations to implement protective algorithms. This paper explores commercial substation automation projects and provides a thorough analysis of how industry leaders have integrated International Electro-technical Commission-61850 (IEC 61850) based CPM in substations. This investigates the IEC 61850 communication protocol's potential for the CPM in constructing contemporary substation automation. A CPM system with software-defined power analytics has great potential for the development of analytics-enabled substation automation, according to the inquiry. A CPM system with software-defined power analytics has great potential for the development of analytics-enabled substation automation, according to the inquiry. The key enablers for reaching the CPM include standardization initiatives in the development and implementation of virtual IED (vIED) software, configuration tools, performance benchmarking, and dynamic, agile compliance testing to ensure interoperability. This study explores these efforts, evaluates their advantages for substation operations, and explores possible future research directions while taking cybersecurity into account.

title: Comparative Analysis of Vanilla Reinforcement Learning vs Q-Learning in Smooth Path Planning Strategies for Mobile Robots
authors: Debaditya Majumdar, Soumya Ranjan Mahapatro, Sudhansu Kumar Samal, Basant Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the real-world scenario, there is an intense need for path-planning robots for various needs. The most used technique to perform path finding is by Vanilla Reinforcement Learning. It is the most basic form of reinforcement learning where the reward system works in a way that the robot finds different paths to find the end point while it keeps on adding the rewards and, in the end, it chooses the path with most rewards. There is also a type of Reinforcement Learning known as Q- Learning. This works on the reward mechanism. As it moves it calculates each episode as a reward or not. This way, it finds the most optimal path to reach its destination. In our paper we are comparing Vanilla Reinforcement Learning and Q-Learning for path planning of a same-dimensional robot. This paper also keeps in mind that the path won't always be straight and there might be various obstructions to find the correct and shortest path. So, we have done a detailed comparative analysis of two algorithms that are Reinforcement Learning and Q-Learning. We have simulated and implemented a Q-Learning algorithm for a mobile robot in a dynamic environment with obstacles and compared it with a MATLAB simulation for Vanilla Reinforcement Learning to understand the differences in their performance in smooth path planning.

title: FedSiaNet: A Privacy Preserving Federated Siamese Network for Real-Time ECG Based Stress Anamoly Detection
authors: Amrita Choudhury, Kandarpa Kumar Sarma, Lachit Dutta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Stress related cardiac abnormalities have become an emerging healthcare concern. Elderly individuals and patients with pre-existing conditions are particularly vulnerable. Therefore, early detection mechanisms are essential for timely medical intervention. Traditional centralized healthcare systems often issue regarding privacy, slow communication and not always adaptable to individual needs. The decentralized, privacy preserving learning methods can solve these limitations. Among them Federated Learning allows many sites to train a model collaboratively without sharing sensitive patient data. It improves privacy and enables personalized heart monitoring even in remote areas. In this study we have present a Federated Siamese Network (FedSiaNet). This is a federated learning system that uses siamese network to detect stress related heart issues from ECG signals. To extract discriminative temporal features from ECG signal pairs each branch of the network uses a temporal convolutional module (TCM), BiLSTM layers and L2 normalization. The model uses contrastive loss to separate normal and stress conditions. FedSiaNet trains the model on data stored across multiple devices, without sharing raw ECG signal. This preserves the patient privacy. The results from the experiments shows the accuracy 97% and ROC-AUC of 0.98. The t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) and principal component analysis (PCA) visualization show the distinct class separation in the embedding space.

title: Cyberbully Detection using Improved RoBERTa and Deep Learning based ResNet50
authors: Archana Gupta, Rajneesh Kumar Singh, Annu Mishra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Cyberbully is one of the most prevalent and dangerous issues nowadays. It is significantly higher in young generations than the older ones. Social media and other common platforms are some key realms of this issue. Background: According to current statistics by UNICEF, every third individual is being bullied on such platforms. More than 50 % students have reported to be cyberbullied and harassed according to the report by Cyberbullying Research Center. The common way of cyberbully are memes. These are the unexplored category of cyberbully detection techniques. Purpose: We need to surveil cyberbully and find a firm method to detect and report such cases at earliest. Methodology: Thus, this article presents one such method to detect the cyberbully using sentiment analysis and emotion recognition. The pre- trained RoBERTa was employed along with BiLSTM followed with ResNet50. Results: The accuracy gained was 96.2 % and F1-Score was observed to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 8 0}$</tex> on MultiBully dataset. Conclusion: The study was carried to use multi-modal data to classify cyberbullying content into more than one type. The findings were compared with prior work that concentrated on analogous tasks, namely in the classification of not-bullying, degrading, insensitive, and violent classes derived on MultiBully dataset

title: A Multi-Modal AI and Blockchain-Based Framework for Automated Academic Certificate Authentication
authors: Deepak Singla, Prakash Tukaram Raut, Shipra Goel, Raju Shanmugam, Deepali Gupta, Reshma R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The rise in educational certificate fraud in India has created significant verification challenges for institutions and employers, as existing manual validation methods are slow, inconsistent, and prone to human error. This paper presents a prototype of a web-based system designed for automated certificate authentication using a multi-modal artificial intelligence pipeline supported by lightweight blockchain hashing. The system integrates Tesseract OCR to extract textual information from uploaded documents and employs a CNN-based model (ResNet) to detect signs of forgery in certificate images. Additionally, each certificate's hash is stored in a shared ledger to ensure tamper-evident verification. Experiments conducted on a dataset of 200 authentic and forged certificates achieved an accuracy of 78% with an average processing time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5$</tex> seconds. The results highlight the potential of AI-driven verification; however, broader datasets and institutional adoption are essential for real-world deployment and improved reliability.

title: Feature Engineering with Long Short-Term Memory Approach for Malicious Risk Identification in Mobile P2P
authors: Sri Sai Krishna Mukkamala
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Malicious actions against mobile peer-to-peer payment systems are increasingly becoming a threat and need to be countered by powerful detection systems. This study presents a feature engineering based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) malicious risk identification with the Risk Activities Dataset 2024 (RBD24). Min-Max Normalization is used to do data preprocessing to address value scaling. Statistical-Behavioural Feature Extraction is used to extract network and user-centric activity patterns and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used to extract features to eliminate redundancy and enhance the efficiency of the model. An LSTM based sequential learning model is used to perform the final classification and is effective to capture the temporal dependence in traffic of the P2P network. Experimental results show an accuracy of 98.7 %, precision of 97.6 %, recall of 97.8 %, and F1-score of 97.8%, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed feature-engineered LSTM framework. This paper shows the usefulness of integrating engineered features with deep temporal models in the security of mobile Peer-to-Peer (P2P) systems.

title: CFD: A Browser-Based Tool for Real-Time Carbon Emission Awareness
authors: Shivam Agarwal, Shubh Arora, Tanvi Sharma, Imran Khan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Climate change is now a normal part of life, so it's important to keep track of and limit your carbon footprint. Every moment we spend in our environment generates carbon in different forms. We generate carbon emissions through activities such as breathing, moving, using electronic devices, and accessing the internet. This paper primarily focuses on the carbon emissions resulting from internet usage. This paper introduces the Carbon Footprint Detector (CFD), a Chrome extension for browsers that uses real-time data analytics to measure the carbon emissions caused by using the internet. The system uses the Chrome API to collect data on network transmissions, uses real-world conversion factors, and gives users session-based metrics like energy use (kWh), carbon emissions (grams <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>), and total environmental impact. The result of the experiment shows that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{20}-\mathbf{300} \text{gms}$</tex> of CO2 is produced by the use of the internet, depending upon the use. The CFD tool has a capability to capture data with 98.5% accuracy and track all the emissions of CO2 by the different activities done by the user, and your current session summary while online. The system was designed to increase people's awareness of environmental issues, which may encourage them to support sustainability policies.

title: Detection of Fraudulant Transaction Over Credit Card Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Ekant Ratanlal Tekam, Afrin Sheikh, Satish D. Kale, Anant Kaulage, Yuvraj S. Suryavanshi, A. S. Sasipriya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The tremendous use of credit card payment that affect to increase in credit card frauds and posing serious risks to financial institutions and customers. Traditional fraud detection methods often struggle with the highly imbalanced nature of transaction data, where fraudulent cases are rare. This paper proposed ensemble learning-based approach to enhance the fraud detection performance over the credit card transactions. The model combines multiple machine learning classifiers such as Random Forest, AdaBoost, K-Nearest Neighbors, and a Deep Neural Network. A publicly available credit card transaction dataset was used, and class imbalance was addressed using SMOTE. The proposed model obtained the accuracy score of 99.94% without SMOTE and improved recall of 87.75 % with SMOTE shows the effectiveness in identifying fraudulent transactions while minimizing false positives. The final outcome of proposed ensemble model shows that when balancing the fraudulent transactions dataset significantly improve the fraud detection performance in real-world financial systems.

title: Real-Time Cyber Threat Intelligence Detection and Response Using Deep Learning for Advanced Threat Classification
authors: Sriram Muganthan G, Srivignesh L, S. Rajashree, A. Jemshia Miriam, M. Nafees Muneera, Santhanakrishnan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper introduces a Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) that is a real-time system, which combines packet-level detection, flow detection using features, machine learning classification, and explainable AI. The system uses the scapy-based packet sniffing to extract the number of connections, rate of services, TCP flag interactions, and protocol metadata and is run against both flow-based analytics and a trained random forest classifier. An adversarial deep autoencoder enhances the process of identifying anomalies due to reconstruction errors, and LIME gives model explainability to security analysts. Streamlit and Flask-SocketIO are used to deliver real-time dashboards and facilitate them to be constantly monitored, dynamically visualized, and blocked on IP. Extra modules compute structured data sets to be used in offline training and model federation, simulating model updates which prevent deterioration of data privacy. It has the capability to detect high-frequency DOS, Probe, R2L, and U2R attacks, and offer transparency, flexibility, and scalability to the current cybersecurity landscape, with the combined architecture.

title: Performance Enhancement of Average Current Control of Boost PFC Converter for Electric Vehicle Charging Applications
authors: Majhrul Israr, Sachin Chauhan, Biranchi Narayan Kar, Amiya Kumar Naik, Nitish Kumar Rajak, Paulson Samuel
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Rectifiers with power factor correction (PFC) are essential in AC/DC conversion systems to achieve a high power factor and low input current distortion. Among various topologies, the boost converter operating with average current control is widely adopted due to its simple structure and effective control characteristics. However, maintaining high power quality and fast output voltage regulation simultaneously remains challenging because the low-frequency ripple (LFR) in the sensed output voltage can distort the reference current, leading to increased harmonics in the input current. Conventional PI controller tuning using the Ziegler-Nichols (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}-\mathbf{N}$</tex>) method often results in suboptimal dynamic reference tracking performance. This paper presents a Bayesian Optimization (BO) based tuning method for the PI current controller to enhance the performance of the inner current loop in a boost PFC converter. The optimized controller parameters significantly improve reference current tracking, yielding better transient and steady-state performance. Simulation results obtained in the MATLAB/Simulink environment demonstrate that the proposed control strategy achieves a low Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), a near-unity power factor, and a well-regulated DC-link voltage, making it highly suitable for electric vehicle (EV) charging applications.

title: A Novel Combined Control Structure for an Isolated Wind System with Reduced Turbine and Battery Sizing
authors: Mitali Mahapatra, Swagat Pati, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents an isolated Wind Energy Conversion System (WECS) integrating a pitch angle-controlled turbine and a battery energy storage system (ESS) to minimize the required capacities of both the generation and storage units. In isolated WECS configurations, the pitch and ESS controllers regulate the same parameter which is the system frequency, often causing instability when operated independently. To address this issue, a priority-based coordinated control algorithm is proposed to determine the respective contributions of pitch and ESS controls in maintaining frequency stability. The system comprises a pitchcontrolled wind turbine coupled with a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) supplying a linear RL load, with the ESS interfaced through a bidirectional converter. Dynamic modeling and simulation are performed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment, and the results confirm that the proposed control strategy effectively maintains system frequency stability under varying wind and load conditions.

title: Design and Fabrication of an Optical Pancreatic Phantom for Fibrosis Assessment
authors: Nithiyaa Dharshini M, Subitcha Jayasankar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Identifying pancreatic fibrosis is challenging as radiographic imaging methods often miss early-stage anatomical and biological changes. Optical spectroscopy techniques, such as Diffuse Reflectance Spectroscopy (DRS), are used to detect cellular-level alterations in early fibrosis; however, they require calibration and standardization to reliably identify fibrosis at an early stage. To calibrate, various phantoms that simulate tissue optical properties exist, but the multilayered structure and inhomogeneity of the pancreas are not accurately represented. To address this, a three-dimensional hydrogel-based phantom that replicates the structural and optical properties of the pancreas is created using a 3D-printed mold that resembles the pancreas. The optical properties are controlled by adding specific amounts of scatterers and absorbers. The optical properties of this multilayer phantom were assessed using an inverse Monte Carlo light transport model with DRS measurements. The absorption and reduced scattering coefficient of the fabricated phantom were within the desired optical property limits of the pancreas, making it an adequate calibration standard for fibrosis detection using DRS.

title: Demonstration of the Diagnostic Potentiality of the Anti-Tetracycline Aptamer on Screen-Printed Electrodes for Detection of Tetracycline
authors: Shuhadah Ismail, Marimuthu Citartan, Amirul Firdaus, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf, Mohd Syamsul, Shaili Falina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Tetracycline (TET) has been used in the poultry sector as a growth promoter. Excessive use of antibiotics in poultry can lead to resistance, reducing the effectiveness of treatment for human illness. This highlights the need for a tool to monitor antibiotic use in poultry. This study focuses on the aptamer-based electrochemical sensor (aptasensor) for detecting TET. The aptasensor was developed by functionalizing the TET-specific aptamer onto gold (Au) working electrode of a printed circuit board electrode (PCBE) and was assessed for TET detection using the differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) technique. The preliminary study shows that the aptasensor is responsive to the presence of the TET, where the peak current of the aptasensor was observed to be decreased to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$117.27 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex> from an average peak of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$142.99 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex> recorded for the bare aptasensor upon exposure to the TET in buffer solution. Furthermore, this study also examines the limit of detection (LOD) of the aptasensor using different TET concentrations. The LOD achieved was 1 mM. Overall, this preliminary study demonstrates the potential of the PCBE aptasensor to be used as a practical antibiotic monitoring tool in the poultry sector.

title: Hybrid Microgrid Stabilization Through Adaptive Virtual Inertia and Nonlinear Compensation-Based Control Architecture
authors: Sarthak Mohanty, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Pravat Kumar Rout, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This manuscript proposes the design of an adaptive virtual inertia-based control scheme (AVICS) employing a nonlinear compensation and synchronization (NCS) method to enhances the voltage and frequency stability in distributed microgrid aspects. The planned control framework successfully compensates abnormal voltage fluctuation, dynamic oscillations, and nonlinear proliferation effects arising from the interface between converters and load variations. The incremental conductance-optimum power algorithm (IC_OPA) offers dynamic tuning of the boost converter modulation index to confirm optimum energy production at an optimal level under varying irradiance and temperature conditions. Allowing virtual inertia path control, additional voltage control has been considered to counteract the oscillatory deviations in the power–voltage response of converter units, which, in turn, offers the steady-state voltage and transient voltage performance. The NCS techniques tunes the voltage signal to compensate the nonlinearity propagation and remarkably reduce the THD at the same time. The developed model has been analyzed broadly in Simulink studies, beginning that the system has better transient performance, quicker synchronization, and harmonics mitigation than conventional droop-based control techniques. Overall, the suggested control paradigm confirmations great promise for enhancing power quality (PQ) and operational reliability as well as energy stability in future microgrids.

title: Leveraging Transfer Learning for Efficient Plant Pathology on Mobile Devices
authors: Samruddhi Sathawane, Shreya Mate, Nikhil Nandanwar, Pranav Gambhire, Sugat Lokhande, Kiran Gavhale
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: World-wide, agriculture plays a significant role in the economy and is the major source of food security. Yet one of the main problems the agriculture sector has to deal with is plant diseases. Consequently, there will be a drop in crop yields and the question of the sustainability of farming will arise. Farmers depending on digital agriculture to minimize losses will have to rely on early disease recognition to take the right measures, make smart decisions, and thus minimize losses. Therefore, the researchers develop a rapid deep learning-based system that is able to pinpoint the plant diseases only by looking at the pictures of leaves. Initially, the researchers employed a binary classification system to differentiate between healthy and unhealthy leaves. In the course of their study, the researchers compared two deep learning models when they used the models of ResNet50 and MobileNetV2. ResNet50 triumphed over MobileNetV2 with an accuracy of 97.08% as opposed to 82.04% for the latter. ResNet50 also showed results of 92.62% and 74%, respectively, in the multiclass disease classification test, indicating that more layers indeed lead to better outcomes. Moreover, a custom CNN model was developed that was able to spot multiple diseases in different plants and crops with a rather impressive accuracy rate of 83.39%, considering the difficulty of the dataset. The researchers decided to create a mobile app that was going to deliver immediate binary predictions on the basis of leaf photos so as to make it ware easier for daily use. All in all, the system proposed is a rapid, precise, and widely accessible remedy that can support the use of intelligent, efficient, and environmentally friendly farming techniques perfectly.

title: Design of Self-Charging Fibrous Power System by Integrating Tribovoltaic Nanogenerator and Supercapacitor
authors: Sikai Zhang, Yunfang Jia
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Electronics, Electrical and Green Energy (AEEGE)
abstract: To address the pressing demand for flexible selfcharging power sources in wearable electronics, this work proposes a fiber-shaped self-charging power system achieved by monolithically integrating a tribovoltaic nanogenerator (TVNG) with a fiber-shaped supercapacitor (FSSC). First, a rigorous theoretical framework is established for this system, applying a quantum mechanical model of the “Bindington” excitation mechanism in the tribovoltaic effect, and analyzing the charge transport mechanism and thermodynamic energy conversion pathways. Subsequently, the theoretical model is validated through coupled multi-physics finite element simulations. The simulation results provide quantitative performance benchmarks, demonstrating that the TVNG can generate stable direct current (DC) output and achieve rectifier-free self-charging of the system. A key advantage of this system is that the DC power converted by the TVNG can be directly stored in the FSSC, eliminating the need for rigid rectifier circuits typically required in conventional self-charging systems. This work provides a validated theoretical and numerical foundation for developing wearable, washable, and biocompatible self-powered textiles, with potential applications in health monitoring, smart fabrics, and the Internet of Things.

title: Decentralized Cloud Storage Systems Using Blockchain Technology
authors: Shafique U Rehman Sahito, Sijjad Ali, Shuaib A Wadho, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Muhammad Waqas Nadeem, Mehran Sayed
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The growing reliance on cloud storage has brought into focus critical concerns related to centralization, data privacy, security, and reliability. Traditional cloud storage systems are often vulnerable to single point failure, data breaches, and high operational costs. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a decentralized cloud storage system that takes advantage of blockchain technology. Blockchain, with its inherent properties of immutability, transparency, and distributed consensus, ensures secure and reliable storage without the need for a central authority. The proposed system incorporates a blockchain-based architecture in which data is encrypted, fragmented, and distributed across multiple nodes in a peer-to-peer network. Smart contracts are used to automate processes such as verification of data integrity, access control, and user authentication, thereby improving operational efficiency and reducing manual intervention. Key features include cryptographic techniques for data privacy, fault tolerance redundancy mechanisms, and a tokenized economy to encourage participation within the network. Experimental results demonstrate the robustness of the system in terms of scalability, data retrieval latency, and cost effectiveness compared to traditional centralized models. The results validate the efficacy of blockchain in addressing trust and transparency issues while ensuring the confidentiality and availability of stored data. This research highlights the potential of decentralized cloud storage systems to revolutionize data management, offering a secure, scalable, and economically viable alternative to traditional models. Future work will explore advanced consensus mechanisms and strategies to further improve system scalability and performance.

title: Vastu Generative Framework (VGF): A Hybrid AI Framework for Vastu-Compliant Architectural Layout Generation
authors: Amit Kumar, Rakshit Malik, Madhumita Mahapatra, Seema Verma, Pridhi Arora
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Architectural design requires knowledge and expertise in space planning, technical and cultural standards. Existing tools are either used to analyze designs that already exists, or to produce layouts without cultural context. This creates a disconnected workflow requiring iterative manual corrections. This paper introduces methodology Vastu Generative Framework (VGF) which is a hybrid Artificial Intelligence (AI) architecture that generates the Vastu compliant architectural plan directly from Natural Language input. The framework includes large language models (LLMs) on requirements extraction, a new Vastu Potential Map on how to convert the expression into continuous suitability landscapes, and a rule-based optimiser of Simulated Annealing to optimise both architectural and Vastu Shastra requirements constraints. Experiments with rectangular, L-shaped and triangular plots have proven that the maximum VGF complies between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85-94 \%$</tex> with zero geometric overlaps where methods based on the baseline yield only between 15 45% compliance. The system produces production ready vector layouts at practical solve times ranging between 30 seconds to 90 seconds with a residential configuration. This means combining traditional's architectural wisdom with this modern, generative artificial intelligence technology; this transformative new workforce means that culturally aware design has become a reality for the user without needing technical expertise from this process or repeated iteration

title: Ensemble Regression Approach to Predict Students' Performance Using Pearson Correlation Feature Selection
authors: Ayesha Ashraf, Mazhar Ali, Shumaim Liaquat, Faiz Nadeem, Qasim Arain
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: The academic performance of students is influenced by multiple demographic, personal, and socioeconomic factors. Predicting student performance is a significant challenge for educators seeking to enhance learning outcomes. This study proposes an ensemble regression approach integrated with Pearson correlationbased feature selection to predict students' exam performance more accurately. The dataset was obtained from Kaggle (“Students' Performance in Exams”), and after preprocessing and one-hot encoding, key features such as gender, parental education, and test preparation courses were selected based on Pearson correlation. Multiple regression models, including Linear Regression, Support Vector Regression (SVR), Decision Tree Regression (DTR), K-Nearest Neighbor Regression (KNNR), and Multilayer Perceptron Regression (MLPR), were trained and compared using Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> score. The best-performing model was MLP Regression (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.08, \text{MSE}=0.013, \mathrm{R}^{2}=0.999$</tex>). The proposed ensemble voting regression model (MLP + LR) further improved accuracy (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.027$</tex>, MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.001, \mathrm{R}^{2}=0.99$</tex>). This demonstrates that combining linear and nonlinear regressors enhances both prediction accuracy and interpretability for predicting student performance.

title: National Data Centres and Sovereign AI Infrastructure: Designing Scalable Platforms for Jordan and Beyond
authors: Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The adoption of large-scale artificial intelligence (AI) models has tightened the link between digital sovereignty and access to high-performance computing infrastructure. For emerging economies like the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan this poses both opportunity and risk. On the one hand, AI promises to transform sectors ranging from fintech and e-commerce to health and logistics, and Jordanian startups are already deploying chatbots, fraud detection and personalised recommendation systems to improve efficiency and customer experience. On the other hand, reliance on foreign hyperscale clouds can undermine data sovereignty, export capital and constrain national innovation. This paper delivers a data-grounded blueprint for a national data centre (NDC) and sovereign AI platform tailored to Jordan's economic and geopolitical context. Our design integrates detailed local metrics-such as the country's eight existing data centres (seven in Amman and one in Aqaba), electricity tariffs for large industry (0.17 USD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\cdot \text{kWh}^{1}$</tex>), renewable energy contributing roughly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27-30 \%$</tex> of generation, the presence of 20 universities offering AI programmes to about 3000 students, and Jordan's commitment to train 15000 people and launch 25 government AI projects by 2027. We present a modular, pod-based architecture with secure sovereign enclaves, high-density AI pods and support for regional service delivery. A total cost of ownership (TCO) model grounded in Jordan-specific data shows that a 50 -rack phase-one facility would require roughly $ 25.7 M in capital expenditure and $ 3.5 M in annual operating expenditure, yielding a five-year TCO around 43.4 M; energy costs dominate long-term OPEX. We examine sensitivity to power-usage-effectiveness (PUE), electricity tariffs and GPU pricing. Finally, we map governance recommendations to existing national bodies defined in the Jordanian AI Strategy and illustrate the platform's impact through local startup case studies, including Repzo's AI-enabled CRM with more than 2,500 representatives and Abjjad's e-reading service with 1.5 million registered users.

title: Numerical Design and Optimization of a Medium Temperature Difference Gamma Stirling Engine for Power Generation Based on Renewable Energy
authors: Kaoutar Laazaar, Noureddine Boutammachte
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: The current environmental situation in the World is very critical and requires an urgent intervention. The best solution to solve this problem is the development of renewable energy. Stirling engine is one of the performant technologies that uses renewable sources. Gamma Stirling engine is one of Stirling engine types that can be built easily and has several advantages. Most works in the field have only focused on studying Gamma Stirling engine with Low Temperature Difference which limits the use of these machines in specific applications. The aim of this study is modeling and optimizing a Medium Temperature Difference Gamma Stirling engine for power production based on biomass as heat source to enlarge the use of this type in other applications. The mathematical model used in this research is the non-ideal adiabatic model. The results of our model were compared and validated against experimental data and previous numerical models. The findings of this paper have revealed that the engine can produce high performance with Medium Temperature Difference.

title:
Ultraviolet Aging Effects on the Dielectric Properties of PVC/Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Nanocomposites Cables Insulation
authors: Sabrina Amraoui, Abdallah Hedir, Ferhat Slimani, Mustapha Moudoud, Omar Lamrous, Ali Durmus
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is extensively employed as an electrical insulating material in high-voltage cable applications, offering reliable dielectric performance and mechanical durability. due to its excellent mechanical and electrical properties. However, under operational conditions, it is exposed to aging factors, particularly UV-radiation, which is one of the most aggressive stressors affecting its stability. The degradation induced by UV exposure leads to structural modifications, loss of mechanical properties, and alterations in dielectric performance. To enhance PVC durability, nanoparticles are commonly incorporated into the polymer matrix to mitigate aging effects. This study investigates the effects of UV aging on the dielectric properties of PVC/Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> nanocomposites. Samples were prepared with varying aluminum oxide (Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) concentrations (0, 2.5 and 5.0 wt.%) and subjected to accelerated UV aging tests. The study involved evaluating dielectric permittivity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex>'), dielectric loss factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tan \delta)$</tex>, and dielectric loss index (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex> “) as a functions of frequency and exposure time. Obtained results shows that adding Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> contributes to the improvement of the dielectric performance of PVC while also acting as a protective agent against UV radiation.

title: Integrated Federated Multimodal AI for Early Cancer Detection Using Radiology, Pathology, and Genomic Signals
authors: Maruf Ahmad, Salman Qavi, Md Mohiuddin, Md Ariful Islam Rana, Md Akhtaruzzaman, Farhana Ferdous
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: In the current study, a combined federated multimodal AI architecture is proposed that is expected to improve the idea of early cancer detection by a combination of radiological, pathological and genomic cues. The architecture uses a federated learning model which enables joint training of models on distributed data sources without compromising the privacy and security of data. Combining multimodal data (radiologic images, e.g., X-rays, CT scans, histopathologic highresolution slides and genomic datasets) in the framework is aimed at enhancing diagnostic accuracy and faster early breast cancer detection. The integrative methodology will allow conducting the analysis of multi-omic data of complex nature in a seamless way without centralizing sensitive patient data, thus complying with privacy standards. Empirical findings indicate that the federated multimodal AI model significantly enhances detection accuracy, sensitivity and specificity of cancer diagnosis. The results indicate that the presented framework could lead to the change of current early cancer detection practices, making the process more accurate, timely, and privacy friendly.

title: Swarm Robotics for Precision Agriculture: Overcoming Coordination Scalability Limits
authors: Loso Judijanto, Sri Nurhayati
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: The rapid evolution of precision agriculture demands innovative approaches to enhance productivity, efficiency, and environmental sustainability. Among these, swarm robotics emerges as a promising paradigm, offering decentralized coordination, fault tolerance, and adaptability for large-scale farming operations. However, significant coordination and scalability limitations continue to hinder its real-world deployment, particularly in heterogeneous and dynamic agricultural environments. This study aims to systematically explore existing strategies to overcome coordination and scalability challenges in swarm robotics for precision agriculture. A Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted using the PRISMA protocol, focusing on peerreviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The research employed a qualitative meta-synthesis approach to extract themes, models, and performance outcomes across multiple studies. Data were sourced exclusively from the ScienceDirect database, covering peer-reviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The search process involved keyword combinations such as “swarm robotics,” “coordination,” “scalability,” and “precision agriculture.” After screening and eligibility checks, 36 articles were selected based on open-access availability and methodological clarity. Thematic analysis was applied to extract coordination strategies, simulation validations, energy management approaches, and integration patterns. Findings indicate that hierarchical coordination models, biologically inspired algorithms (e.g., ACO, PSO), and hybrid communication protocols are the most effective in addressing scalability.

title: Tracking the Dieback of Palm and Olive Groves in The Skoura Oase (Morocco): A Spatio-Temporal Approach Using Remote Sensing and Machine Learning
authors: Youssef Lassiane, Farid El Wahidi, Hoda Benazun, Hassan Ait Naceur
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Desertification remains one of the most pressing environmental challenges in arid and semi-arid regions, undermining ecosystem resilience and agricultural productivity. In southern Morocco, oasis landscapes constitute highly fragile socio-ecological systems where vegetation dieback has intensified under the combined influence of climatic stress and human pressure. This study explores the spatio-temporal dynamics of palm and olive tree dieback in the Skoura Oasis (Morocco) from 2015 to 2025, using multi-temporal Sentinel-2 imagery processed on the Google Earth Engine (GEE) platform. A Random Forest algorithm was applied to tasseled cap transformation (TCT) components-Brightness, Greenness, and Wetness-to extract ecological indicators related to vegetation vigor and surface moisture. The model achieved an overall accuracy exceeding 89%, confirming its robustness in discriminating vegetation health conditions. Results indicate a marked acceleration in palm and olive dieback during the last decade, with the southern sections of the palmeraie exhibiting the most severe losses. In the central oasis, a persistent and continuous dieback pattern was also detected. These findings reveal the alarming pace of ecosystem deterioration across the Draa oases, reflecting the second agro-ecological crisis driven by progressive aridification and groundwater depletion. This research demonstrates the high potential of integrating Sentinel-based remote sensing with machine learning for long-term ecological monitoring, providing critical insights for sustainable oasis management and the preservation of traditional agroforestry systems in dryland environments.

title: Enhancing Precision Agriculture With Lightweight Object Detection of Date-Palm Flowers
authors: Thowayba Elkaffash, Toqa Alremawi, Alya Al-Adbah, Mohamed Sultan Mohamed Ali, Asan G.A. Muthalif
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: Agricultural production remains one of the most labor-intensive sectors, where repetitive and physically demanding operations are often carried out under harsh conditions. In date-palm cultivation, many critical tasksparticularly pollination-are still performed manually at considerable heights, posing safety risks and limiting scalability. While computer vision has advanced automation in harvesting and disease monitoring, flower identification for pollination remains largely underexplored. This paper presents a comparative study of lightweight deep learning models for datepalm flower detection, aimed at enabling real-time autonomous pollination under constrained hardware. Three models: YOLOv8n, YOLOv8s, and RT-DETR-I were evaluated under identical conditions, comparing their detection accuracy, inference speed, and computational efficiency. Results showed that YOLOv8n scored the fastest detection, while YOLOv8s achieved the best balance between speed and size as well as the highest recall. RT-DETR-1 provided the highest accuracy in mAP95 scores, but at a significantly higher computational cost. The findings highlight the trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency and offer practical insights into selecting perception models for vision-based robotic pollination systems.

title: Mathematical Modelling and Drying Kinetic Assessment of Coconut Meat Under Infrared Drying
authors: Jack O. Bertulfo, Eddie A. Carmelotes, June Kierlynne M. Beltran
publish: 3rd 2025 IEEE International Conference on Agrosystem Engineering, Technology &amp; Applications (AGRETA)
abstract: This study evaluated the drying behavior and kinetics of coconut meat using a cabinet-type infrared dryer as a modern alternative to traditional sun and kiln drying. Recognizing the economic importance of coconut products and the quality issues in conventional drying, an Arduino-controlled infrared dryer was designed and tested for thin-layer drying at Agusan del Sur State College of Agriculture and Technology. Experiments used three drying temperatures <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(80^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 90^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$</tex> at a fixed 100 mm irradiation distance. Moisture content and drying time were measured, revealing that higher temperatures significantly reduced drying duration: 180 minutes at 80° C, 150 minutes at 90° C, and 135 minutes at 100° C. Among five mathematical models, the Midilli model best described the drying kinetics, with the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{R}^{2}(0.9986)$</tex> and lowest RMSE values for drying temperature of 90° C and 100° C. Findings conclude that infrared drying, particularly at 90° C, is an efficient method for producing high-quality copra and suitable for industrial optimization.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on Cybersecurity Education: Trends, Challenges, and the Role of AI
authors: Yassine Mouhssine, Hicham Boutracheh, Amine Sadik, Aniss Moumen
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Cybersecurity education has become a crucial area of focus as cyber threats continue to evolve rapidly. This study presents a systematic literature review exploring the current trends, challenges, and advancements in cybersecurity education, with a particular emphasis on the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity competitions. To ensure a comprehensive analysis, we retrieved more than 500 articles from Scopus, of which over 298 were identified as directly relevant to our study. Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions, cyber ranges, and other hands on learning environments have emerged as essential tools for bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical cybersecurity skills. However, challenges such as scalability, accessibility, and real-time skill assessment persist. AI is increasingly being integrated into these competitions to enhance personalized learning, automate challenge generation, and provide intelligent feedback to participants. This review examines how AI-driven methodologies contribute to improving cybersecurity education by offering adaptive learning experiences, optimizing competition structures, and facilitating real-time performance evaluation. By synthesizing insights from recent research, this study identifies existing gaps and proposes future directions for leveraging AI in cybersecurity competitions to better equip students for real-world cyber defense scenarios.

title: Scalable and Explainable Heart Disease Prediction on Imbalanced Data
authors: Mayssa Ben Fekih, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Heart disease remains a leading cause of mortality worldwide. With the increasing availability of digital health records, machine learning has emerged as a promising approach to facilitate early diagnosis. However, traditional deep learning methods often suffer from lengthy processing times, limited interpretability, and dependence on complex data formats, such as ECG signals. In this study, we present a practical and interpretable machine-learning framework for heart disease prediction. Our approach integrates Spark Streaming for scalable real-time data processing, LightGBM for efficient classification, SHAP for feature-level interpretability, and SMOTE to address class imbalance. We benchmark our method against recent deep learning and ensemble-based models. Experiments conducted on the large-scale Heart_2020_cleaned dataset from Kaggle demonstrate that our framework achieves high accuracy and AUC scores while maintaining fast execution and clear interpretability, making it a strong candidate for deployment in real- world clinical environments.

title: Cryptocurrency Volatility Forecasting Using Garch, LSTM, Hybrid GARCH-LSTM and Hybrid CNN-LSTM with Intraday Frequency
authors: Houssemeddine Jlassi, Haykel Hamdi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Risk is a key concept in the world of finance. Measuring and predicting this critical aspect is therefore essential. Our paper compares three volatility prediction techniques: a traditional GARCH <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,1)$</tex> model, a machine learning model and two hybrid models: GARCH (1,1)-LSTM and CNN-LSTM. In contrast to the existing literature, our results show the invalidity of the hybrid models, which is outperformed by the traditional GARCH model and the LSTM model. This is explained by the slow reactivity and poor tail risk capture of these models. Thus, we note that the LSTM model is the most adequate and reliable for all subsamples.

title: Rest Allowance Models for Optimizing Task Scheduling and Worker Well-Being in Industry
authors: Dorsaf Aribi, Olfa Belkahla Driss, Hind Bril El Haouzi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Rest Allowance (RA) models have long served as essential tools for managing worker fatigue, optimizing task scheduling, and ensuring sustainable productivity in industrial environments. This paper provides a comprehensive review of existing RA models, encompassing traditional approaches based on standardized time allowances, ergonomic assessments, and more recent developments that account for dynamic fatiguerecovery patterns. We also identify key limitations that hinder their effectiveness in modern, human-centric manufacturing systems. These include the lack of personalization, limited integration of mental fatigue, and insufficient consideration of task sequencing and inter-worker dependencies. The paper concludes by highlighting the need for adaptive, personalized RA models that integrate physical and cognitive demands to enhance wellbeing and efficiency in reconfigurable production systems.

title: An Intelligent Dimension-Reduction Method for Emergency-Supplier Evaluation Indicators Based on Filter-Wrapper and PSO-BP
authors: Jiayi Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: To address high-dimensional, redundant evaluation indicators and strong subjectivity in expert scoring within emergency-supply-chain contexts, an intelligent dimension-reduction model is proposed that integrates Filter-Wrapper feature selection with a PSO-BP neural network. An information-gain ratio is first employed for rapid preliminary screening, followed by hybrid recursive feature elimination that ensembles SVM, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting Machines for refined selection. Particle swarm optimization (PSO) is then applied to globally optimize the weights and thresholds of a BP neural network, yielding an adaptive evaluation model. Experiments on data from 93 suppliers indicate that the indicator dimensionality is compressed from 27 to 17 core features. With the PSO-BP approach, the model achieves a prediction accuracy of 94.6% and a recall of 93.3%, significantly outperforming SVM, Random Forest and the standard BP model. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of machine-learning techniques in supply-chain risk assessment and provide a reusable technical framework for indicator optimization in high-dimensional, small-sample scenarios.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection of Depression from Social Media Text
authors: Abhay Bairagi, Anu Sayal, Saurabh Singh, Avisha Gupta, Indu Bala, Madhuri Gokhale, Dinker Dubey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: The increasing importance of early depression identification using social media text is one of the main uses of artificial intelligence in digital mental health. However, existing Deep Learning models frequently suffer from overfitting, interpretability issues, and a lack of emotional context, particularly when affective cues like emojis are removed. This study introduces a hybrid lexical-contextual ensemble that combines DistilBERT and TF-IDF + Logistic Regression to capture both lexical and semantic data. By using a weighted integration approach to balance the trade-off between bias and variance, the ensemble minimizes false negatives by almost half when compared to individual models, achieving an F1 score of 0.866 and a ROC-AUC of 0.9385. In order to preserve affectrich information typically lost with standard text normalization, an emotionally intelligent preprocessing pipeline is also provided, which distinguishes emojis from their semantic equivalents. The system further emphasizes computational efficiency by using DistilBERT, which requires only forty percent of BERT's parameters, which is suitable for lowresource settings. Empirical results from the Kaggle Twitter Depression Dataset prove that the introduced framework surpasses current single-model baselines that are interpretable, lightweight, and computationally efficient methods of early depression identification. However, current transformer-based models have issues with interpretability and efficiency. This study investigates this gap with a hybrid lexical-contextual ensemble framework that balances accuracy, explainability, and computational cost.

title: AgriShield: A Hybrid NLP and Machine Learning Framework for Plant Disease Prediction
authors: Ritik Saini, Vikas Maurya, Shubham Singh, Tarun Shukla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: Plant diseases continue to be a significant threat to the world's food security system, and crops are lost yearly. Conventional diagnosis approaches require professional intervention and are not always available to smallholder farmers in developing countries. The paper presents AgriShield, a unified system using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) to predict plant diseases. The farmers can enter textual queries describing the symptoms or submit images of the crops, and AgriShield guesses at the most likely disease and preventive and remedial actions. The system uses transformer-based NLP models to extract symptoms and ensemble classifiers, utilising textual and visual input. Some of the experimental outputs of AgriShield on benchmark datasets indicate that AgriShield has an accuracy of 90 %, which is better than textonly and image-only systems.

title: Smart Energy Communities: Business Models and P2P Trading for Sustainable Power
authors: Muhammad Waseem, Khizer Aftab, Muhammad Waleed Asghar, Waqas Asghar, Amy Fahy, Fabiano Pallonetto
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Innovations in Information and Communication Technologies (IICT)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of various off-grid and grid-connected hybrid renewable energy systems (HRES) to support the development of smart energy communities in the municipal committee (MC) Burewala, district Vehari, Pakistan. The study aims to improve residential electrification through cost-effective and sustainable power solutions, while enabling future integration of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading mechanisms within community-based energy networks. Centering on techno-economic performance, the Hybrid Optimization Model for Electric Renewable (HOMER) Pro software is employed to design, simulate, and optimize multiple HRES configurations. Sensitivity parameters including nominal discount rate, inflation rate, and annual capacity shortage are examined to refine the net present cost (NPC) and levelized cost of electricity (LCOE). Results indicate that the grid-connected configuration comprising solar photovoltaic (PV), diesel generators (DG), and battery storage yields the highest cost efficiency, with an NPC of 1,677,396 USD and an LCOE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.02519 \text{USD} / \text{kWh}$</tex>. To evaluate the robustness of this solution, a sensitivity analysis is performed to verify its proximity to the global minimum. Finally, the study discusses key social, technical, and economic barriers that may affect large-scale deployment of smart energy communities and P2P trading frameworks, and offers recommendations to facilitate their successful implementation in sustainable energy systems.

title: Inventory Demand Forecasting Using Cat Boost Regression: A Machine Learning Method
authors: Elluru Bhavana, A. Vyshnavi, Sugandha Saxena, B.S. Likitha Sreeja, C. Druthi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Sales forecasting plays a important role in the part of retail management and decision making. This research uses past operations and data. The dataset contain numerical and categorical attributions that shows different operations. The workflow involves the preprocessing, like handling missing values, removing duplications, and conducting EDA to find patterns and collaborations. Ww train regression algorithms like Linear regression, Lasso, Ridge and XGBoost regressor to identify most efficient model. We use model performance using Mean Absolute Error(MAE). Experimental results show ensemble based models like XGBoost, outperform traditional regression methods: they give lower prediction errors and better generalization. This work implemented machine learning techniques gives a useful framework for modeling sales dynamics and significantly improve inventory management, demand planning and business decision support in the retail sector.

title: Brown Bear Optimization-Based Error Minimization for Reduced-Order Modeling of Off-Grid Microgrids
authors: Richa Chaudhary, V. P. Singh, Akhilesh Mathur
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Microgrid systems are the interconnection of various subsystems that consist of distributed energy resources, energy storage units, and loads that integrate to form complex higher-order systems (HOSs). However, assessing and controlling such complex HOSs shows significant challenges. To address these issues, reduced-order models (ROMs) are proposed to solve the HOS while conserving its essential system characteristics. In this paper, an approximation of the off-grid microgrid (OM) system is developed by employing the time moments (TMs) and Markov parameters (MPs). The proposed off-grid microgrid reduced-order model (OMROM) for the off-grid microgrid higher-order system (OMHOS) is obtained by minimizing the errors between the TMs and MPs of the OMHOS and its OMROM. For this error minimization, an objective function is developed and enhanced using the brown bear optimization (BBO) algorithm. The suggested OMROM is obtained under the criteria of the Hurwitz stability criterion and steady-state matching conditions. The effectiveness and applicability of the developed OMROM for representing the OMHOS are demonstrated through tabular analyses and graphical responses.

title: HAR70+W: Cross-Dataset Alignment of Thigh Back and Wrist Sensors for Elderly Activity Monitoring
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: We present HAR70+W, a cross-dataset fusion approach for elderly activity monitoring that combines complementary inertial signals from two public sources: HAR70 + (thigh/back) sensors and WEDA-Fall wrist-worn signals. The proposed pipeline performs cross-dataset alignment and window-based segmentation to handle differences in sampling characteristics and recording protocols before learning activity representations. We benchmark several deep architectures, including 1D-CNN,LSTM,CNN+BiLSTM, Transformer, and a hybrid CNN+Transformer model. We introduce window based signal segmentation to enhance the performance of the recognition process. The model based on CNN+Transformer shows a considerable advantage over others at 90.62% accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.867. This framework is an attempt to overcome these problems, as shown by heterogeneous sampling rates and label alignment, and demonstrates that hybrid sensor fusion with advanced architectures has the potential to make a difference in practical elderly care use cases.

title: MIMO-Enabled Robust Communication Architecture for Fault-Tolerant Smart Grids Under Renewable Uncertainties
authors: Shubhalaxmi Mohapatra, Satyadeep Das, Debasis Nanda, Bibhu Prasad Sahoo, Sandeep Gupta, Kamalakanta Shaw, Priyanka Jethi, Subhendu Sekhar Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy generation and the increasing dependence on cyber-physical communication infrastructures have introduced new challenges to the reliability of modern smart grids. Renewable sources such as solar and wind exhibit stochastic variability that leads to rapid changes in power system operating conditions, resulting in electromagnetic interference, transient disturbances, and communication bottlenecks during critical events. Conventional single-input single-output (SISO) communication links often fail to maintain the latency, throughput, and outage performance required for protection, control, and wide-area monitoring. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a MIMO-enabled robust communication architecture designed specifically for smart grid environments subject to renewable intermittency and grid faults. The framework integrates multi-antenna transmission, adaptive switching between spatial diversity and spatial multiplexing, CSIaware real-time beamforming, and spatial interference suppression. A grid-event-driven adaptation mechanism is introduced, enabling the communication system to reconfigure transmission parameters based on fault severity, renewable ramp rates, and instantaneous channel conditions. Comprehensive simulations using MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate significant improvements in BER, SNR stability, outage probability, latency during protection events, and throughput during renewable ramp disturbances compared to SISO, OFDM, and fixed MIMO systems.
